Our Philosophy

A Safe Space for Anxiety and Trauma Therapy

At BODHI MINDCARE, therapy is designed to help you understand yourself more deeply and work through emotional challenges at a manageable pace.

Our Philosophy

A Safe Space for Anxiety and Trauma Therapy

At BODHI MINDCARE, therapy is designed to help you understand yourself more deeply and work through emotional challenges at a manageable pace.

Our Philosophy

A Safe Space for Anxiety and Trauma Therapy

At BODHI MINDCARE, therapy is designed to help you understand yourself more deeply and work through emotional challenges at a manageable pace.

01

Compassion

Every session begins and ends with unconditional positive regard for the whole person — creating space for honesty, growth, and understanding.

02

Evidence

We use only clinically validated approaches, so your time is never wasted on uncertainty or guesswork, only on meaningful progress.

03

Inclusion

A safe, affirming space for people of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences, where you feel seen and supported.

04

Accessibility

Sliding scale rates, flexible scheduling, and in-person or virtual options designed to meet your needs comfortably.

Kind words

Real words from real people

Read full stories

Featured story

Individual Therapy

"Dr. Harvee Shah understood my trauma in a way no other therapist had before. The EMDR work we did together gave me my life back. I can't overstate what that means."

Rachel C.

Read full story

About this journey

Rachel came to BODHI MINDCARE after years of living with unresolved trauma. She'd tried other approaches before — talk therapy, self-help, medication — but nothing had reached the root. Working with Dr. Harvee Shah over six months, Rachel engaged in an intensive EMDR protocol that gradually allowed her to process what had felt unprocessable. "I was terrified the first day," she told us. "By the end, I felt like I'd finally put down something I'd been carrying for fifteen years."

Featured story

About this journey

Rachel came to BODHI MINDCARE after years of living with unresolved trauma. She'd tried other approaches before — talk therapy, self-help, medication — but nothing had reached the root. Working with Dr. Harvee Shah over six months, Rachel engaged in an intensive EMDR protocol that gradually allowed her to process what had felt unprocessable. "I was terrified the first day," she told us. "By the end, I felt like I'd finally put down something I'd been carrying for fifteen years."

Rachel C.
